memory.summary: My plan to finish the analysis first feels rigid, but joining cohort_3 for lunch aligns better with my priority on relationships. I'll need to reschedule the reading later.

desires: Stay connected with cohort_3 after lunch so the moment doesn't feel like a missed opportunity, even if it means pushing my reading to tomorrow.
Avoid any awkwardness or tension with the professor regarding the rescheduled reading by making sure I look committed when we eventually meet up again.
Feel genuinely present and relaxed during the next meal rather than rushing back to work before everyone else has finished eating.

goals.description: Join cohort_3 for lunch immediately to solidify the connection before it fades, accepting that the assignment analysis will have to wait until tomorrow.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) outweighs academic urgency here. Missing this moment with my closest friends feels like a high social cost, whereas delaying the 'soft' deadline work by one day is manageable given my deliberate planning style.

goals.description: Eat slowly and fully present during lunch, resisting the urge to mentally check off tasks or rush back to the desk once the meal ends.
goals.rationale: Feeling genuinely relaxed aligns with my desire to avoid stress and maintain physical stamina. Rushing would create unnecessary friction in the relationship dynamic I'm trying to nurture.

agent.last_reasoning: Going with cohort_3 aligns with my top affiliation priority (0.5). Delaying the 'soft' deadline work is manageable given my deliberate planning style, whereas missing this lunch would damage trust. I'll eat fully present to avoid friction.
